trivia_bullets.text: The Kor of Zendikar are nomadic cliff-dwellers who are masters of ropes and hooks, using them for travel, hunting, and spirituality. A hooked line is a sacred symbol representing connection to each other and to the world. They 'rarely use unreliable devices such as crossbows to propel their grappling hooks' — skill of arm over mechanism. [WebSearch: Kor MTG Wiki via coolstuffinc ecology article] The flavor text — 'With rope, I can fly as far as the rope can reach. But with light, the sky is the limit.' — captures this philosophy: tool-mastery as foundation, light/spirit as transcendence. [Scryfall]
sources: WebSearch: Ecology of the Kor, CoolStuffInc

trivia_bullets.text: Kor Sky Climber is a 3/2 Kor Soldier Ally for 2W with a pay-1W activated flying ability. The stat line is aggressively costed for white commons; the flying activation makes it a threat that demands an answer. Pauper-legal and penny-legal. [Scryfall] EDHREC rank 28868 reflects its role as a draft staple rather than a Commander card.

Art by Victor Adame Minguez, a Spanish illustrator with extensive MTG credits. This is one of his OGW commissions. [Scryfall] The sunset color palette (deep purple, warm orange) against the Kor's pale skin and glowing golden energy lines is a visual signature of the Zendikar art direction in this block.
